buginformation.summary: Unnecessary file into byte[] copy implementation in eclipse.jdt.debug
buginformation.description: In JavaStackTraceConsole and JavaAppletLaunchConfigurationDelegate there are unnecessary code which copies into a byte array from a File, instead of using the built in java.nio.file.Files APIs.

buginformation.summary: Add ability to label objects during debugging
buginformation.description: It is pretty hard to quickly identify objects and track them during the debugging session, because there could be hundred similar instances, and trying to remember the unique id, is not a too pleasant experience - and the human brain - at least mine - is pretty limited in this area. It would be much better, if we could attach label to various objects, and if that object instances show up in the Variables/Expressions view, these labels could be displayed so it will stand out.

buginformation.summary: Export and import of breakpoints don't work properly for record
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 287324 project
1) Import the project attached 2) Import the breakpoint attached. 3) Export all breakpoints 4) Import all breakpoints
2 breakpoints are missing after step 4

buginformation.summary: Conditional breakpoints fail when recursive generics are involved
[releng] AbstractReader in the JDI tests uses CR as line endings
buginformation.description:
org.eclipse.jdt.debug.jdi.tests/tests/org/eclipse/debug/jdi/tests/AbstractReader.java uses single CRs as line endings. (Not CR-LF!)
For git, this is a binary file.
I suggest to normalize this to use LF as line endings.

buginformation.summary: [Test] testBug573547_insideLambda_onOuterScopeVariable_whileOnPreviousFrame failing on windows
buginformation.description: Follow up from https://bugs.eclipse.org/bugs/show_bug.cgi?id=573547#c5

buginformation.summary: Highlight labelled objects in the "Expressions" view too
buginformation.description: Currently JDIModelPresentation doesnt return foreground color for IWatchExpression and JavaInspectExpression, so if the user labels an object, they wont get highlighted. Unfortunately, the 'Variables' and the 'Expressions' view use different logic to return the 'Value' of a variable, so we can't easily add the label there.

buginformation.summary: Expressions does not work when records defined as inner classes are used
Exception processing async thread queue
buginformation.description:
Trying to debug a Nebula snippets creates an exception. See below
java.lang.ArrayIndexOutOfBoundsException: Index -1 out of bounds for length 0 at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.debug.eval.ast.engine.EvaluationSourceGenerator.getCompleteSnippet(EvaluationSourceGenerator.java:152) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.debug.eval.ast.engine.EvaluationSourceGenerator.(init)(EvaluationSourceGenerator.java:70) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.debug.eval.ast.engine.ASTEvaluationEngine.getCompiledExpression(ASTEvaluationEngine.java:372) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.debug.eval.ast.engine.ASTEvaluationEngine.evaluate(ASTEvaluationEngine.java:154) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.debug.ui.JavaWatchExpressionDelegate$EvaluationRunnable.run(JavaWatchExpressionDelegate.java:153) at org.eclipse.jdt.internal.debug.core.model.JDIThread$ThreadJob.run(JDIThread.java:3436) at org.eclipse.core.internal.jobs.Worker.run(Worker.java:63)
Tested with: Eclipse SDK Version: 2021-12 (4.22) Build id: I20211108-1800 OS: Windows 10, v.10.0, x86_64 / win32 Java vendor: Eclipse Foundation Java runtime version: 11.0.12+7 Java version: 11.0.12

buginformation.summary: Allow debugger to disallow final fields modifications
buginformation.description:
Bug 94223 opened final fields for modifications in Eclipse debugger. For some obscure reasons JVM allows debugger to do that, and that allows fun for experienced Java developers.
However, in the real world we might have cases where the consequences of the change are not trivial and could affect seemingly unrelated code.
We have custom product that uses extends JDT debugger. Customers can debug their code and because of bug 94223 they can change final fields *without* noticing they do something that is considered to be used by advanced users and only if the change is "safe".
So they debug application to some point where they want to change a Boolean value embedded in some domain object. They *think* they change the domain object state only, but because the same Boolean.TRUE instance is shared with gazillion of other objects, application explodes.
Below a trivial code that demonstrates the failed assumption after changing value of a Boolean.
public class FinalBoolean { public static void main(String[] args) { Boolean b1 = Boolean.valueOf("true"); System.out.println(b1); // true // On breakpoint on the line below change b1.value to true. Boolean b2 = Boolean.valueOf("false"); System.out.println(b2); // false System.out.println(b1); // false !!! System.out.println("'true' equals 'false'? " + b1.equals(b2)); // true ! } }
Same applies to everything else that has final fields, but especially Boolean, Integer Long are very dangerous because they are widely used and cached by default.
There are few things we could improve:
1) We should allow to disallow change of final fields in debugger 2) We should allow debuggers that extend default JDT debugger and reuse "org.eclipse.jdt.debug” model to provide their own "variableValueEditor" via "org.eclipse.debug.ui.variableValueEditors". 3) We should warn if someone tries change final fields
I plan to propose two patches for 1) and 2) that would introduce a UI preference and allow custom Java debuggers to overwrite "variableValueEditor" contribution from JDT to implement more sophisticated logig regarding debug value changes.

buginformation.summary: Possibility to navigate to field declaration
buginformation.description: During debugging, for a variable, currently it is possible to navigate to the actual or to the declared type - with the "Open Actual Type" and "Open Declared Type" context menu item - but it's not possible to navigate to the declaration of the field - and if the class hierarchy is deep, finding the actual declaring class is not always trivial. It would be nice, if there were an action to navigate there.

buginformation.summary: [18] Add Execution Environment for JavaSE-18
buginformation.description: There should be an Execution Environment for JavaSE-18 in the Execution Environment Preference page.

buginformation.summary: [18] Keep merging from master to BETA branch
buginformation.description: Beta needs a periodic update

buginformation.summary: [18] Java 18 API doc to be added for JRE library
buginformation.description: Java doc location should be added for new Java 18 release. This will be available only after Java 18 GA.

buginformation.summary: [18] Remove JCP disclaimers and BETA from code in Debug
buginformation.description: Remove JCP disclaimers from code in Debug.

buginformation.summary: Evaluations for lambda variables are not working on nested lambda
Setting label on nulls and primitive types does not work/make sense
buginformation.description: The "Set Object Label..." action is visible even for nulls and for primitive types. For primitive values, the dialog wont come up - as there is an explicit check, that 'value instanceof IJavaObject', however it shows up for nulls. But in the end, labeling nulls wont make any sense. It would be better, if this action is visible only for non-null objects.

buginformation.summary: Deadlock situation when adding new JRE
buginformation.description:
Created attachment 288061 Thread-Dump
While adding a new JDK beside an existing one, I ended up with a deadlock.
- Menu: Window - Preferences - Java - Installed JRE - Add - Select my JDK Folder - Press "Apply"
See the attached treaddump!
The "main"-thread and "Worker-60: Update Installed JREs"
are waiting for lock
0x000000070f611540
This is locked by thread "Worker-52: Update Installed JREs"
As default OpenJDK 11 was already in the JRE list. I added JDK8.
After the Eclipse Restart, the JDK8 was successfully added.

buginformation.summary: NPE with breakpoint in ClassLoader.getDefinedPackage()
Breakpoints cannot be place in nested lambdas
Cannot create lambda entry breakpoints on same method on 2 different lines
Lambda entry breakpoint does not suspend while debugging
LineTrackerTests.testFlood fails since I20220326-1800
buginformation.description:
https://download.eclipse.org/eclipse/downloads/drops4/I20220327-1800/testresults/html/org.eclipse.jdt.debug.tests_ep424I-unit-cen64-gtk3-java11_linux.gtk.x86_64_11.html
LineTrackerTests testFlood Failure Wrong number of lines expected:(10000) but was:(5740)
junit.framework.AssertionFailedError: Wrong number of lines expected:(10000) but was:(5740) at junit.framework.Assert.fail(Assert.java:57) at junit.framework.Assert.failNotEquals(Assert.java:329) at junit.framework.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:78) at junit.framework.Assert.assertEquals(Assert.java:234) at junit.framework.TestCase.assertEquals(TestCase.java:377) at org.eclipse.jdt.debug.tests.core.LineTrackerTests.testFlood(LineTrackerTests.java:170) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke0(Native Method) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.NativeMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(NativeMethodAccessorImpl.java:62) at java.base/jdk.internal.reflect.DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.invoke(DelegatingMethodAccessorImpl.java:43) at java.base/java.lang.reflect.Method.invoke(Method.java:566) at junit.framework.TestCase.runTest(TestCase.java:177) at junit.framework.TestCase.runBare(TestCase.java:142) at org.eclipse.jdt.debug.tests.AbstractDebugTest.runBare(AbstractDebugTest.java:2679) at junit.framework.TestResult$1.protect(TestResult.java:122) at junit.framework.TestResult.runProtected(TestResult.java:142) at junit.framework.TestResult.run(TestResult.java:125) at junit.framework.TestCase.run(TestCase.java:130) at junit.framework.TestSuite.runTest(TestSuite.java:241) at junit.framework.TestSuite.run(TestSuite.java:236) at junit.framework.TestSuite.runTest(TestSuite.java:241) at org.eclipse.jdt.debug.tests.DebugSuite$1.run(DebugSuite.java:61) at java.base/java.lang.Thread.run(Thread.java:829)
Interestingly, I'm unable to execute testst locally, the launch config in the test doesn't start at all.
